sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The pair moved right by 4 columns (from 12-13/14-17 to 16-17/18-21). The target is at cols 28-29. The pair needs to move 12 more columns right (3 more steps).
But wait, the level isn't complete yet. Let me check if the alignment is correct. The pair's N strip is at cols 16-17, and the target N segment is at cols 28-29. They're not aligned yet.
I need 3 more clicks of the bottom blue to move the pair to cols 28-29.
But wait, I also notice the middle white region (rows 24-39) disappeared. And there's no white region at rows 24-39 anymore. The blue block at rows 36-39 is still there but the white region it was adjacent to is gone.
Let me continue moving the pair right.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- Pair at cols 16-17 (N) / 18-21 (c). Target at cols 28-29.
- Need 3 more RIGHT moves (12 columns) to align.
- Bottom blue moves pair RIGHT by 4 columns per click.
Plan: Click bottom blue 3 more times to reach cols 28-29.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Click bottom blue 3 more times to align pair with target
predict(expect_change=True, note='Moving pair right 3 more steps to cols 28-29')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2}] * 3)

print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("\n=== SMALL OBJECTS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
else:
    print("Level not yet complete")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
